title: dalbergia cumingiana
intro: Dalbergia cumingiana, also known as Philippine rosewood or Molave, is a deciduous tree species native to the Philippines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Dalbergia cumingiana include its ability to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the plant's wood, bark, and leaves can be used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that are used to treat various health conditions.
